steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The passage structure uses "one sibling… the other sibling" to compare traits—thin lips versus full lips. This parallel construction signals a direct contrast between what two siblings inherit.

steps.title: Apply the transition to the contrast
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast — one sibling inherits thin lips, the other inherits the opposite (full lips). The blank must mark that direct opposition within the same "For instance" example.
  • Choice A — by contrast,: signals direct opposition between the two siblings' traits, fitting the thin-vs.-full-lips comparison. ✓
  • Choice B — for example,: introduces a new specific instance of a general claim; the blank already sits inside a "For instance" example, so nesting another example is illogical. ✗
  • Choice C — with this in mind,: signals that a conclusion or action follows from prior reasoning; the second sibling's trait is not a response to any reasoning. ✗
  • Choice D — lastly,: signals the final item in a sequence, but there is no sequential list here — only a two-way comparison. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
